texto: Then the soul, freed from vice, purged by studies of true philosophy, versed in spiritual life, and practised in matters of the intellect, devoted to the contemplation of her own substance, as if awakened from deepest sleep, opens those eyes which all possess but few use, and sees in herself a ray of that light which is the true image of the angelic beauty communicated to her, and of which she then communicates a faint shadow to the body.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Baldassarre Castiglione: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “Then the soul, freed from vice, purged by studies of true philosophy, versed in spiritual life, a…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, christianity, courtier pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de courtier e light.

frase_original: Then the soul, freed from vice, purged by studies of true philosophy, versed in spiritual life, and practised in matters of the intellect, devoted to the contemplation of her own substance, as if awakened from deepest sleep, opens those eyes which all possess but few use, and sees in herself a ray of that light which is the true image of the angelic beauty communicated to her, and of which she then communicates a faint shadow to the body.

texto: Outward beauty is a true sign of inner goodness. This loveliness, indeed, is impressed upon the body in varying degrees as a token by which the soul can be recognized for what it is, just as with trees the beauty of the blossom testifies to the goodness of the fruit.
explicacao: Citação de Baldassarre Castiglione sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Outward beauty is a true sign of inner goodness. This loveliness, indeed, is impressed upon the b…” articula reflexao, beauty, goodness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e reflexao.
